sections.a_description: The distinctive visual language of Three.js Generative — combining 3d, three.js, advanced js elements to create interfaces that are immediately recognizable and purposefully designed. Every color, font choice, and spacing decision works together to evoke the specific mood and cultural context that defines this aesthetic, from the primary accent color to the carefully chosen typography.

This style finds its natural home across websites and applications that value 3d and three.js design principles. You'll see it on creative portfolios, brand microsites, specialized platforms, and digital experiences where the visual language itself communicates as powerfully as the content — proving that the right aesthetic choice can transform a standard layout into a memorable experience.

a_image_cover.alt: Three.js Generative style use case illustration
Three.js Generative style use case illustration
Three.js Generative style use case illustration

sections.a_description: The distinctive visual aesthetic of Three.js Generative — a design approach that creates unique, memorable interfaces. Background: dark (#080818). The .inner panel has a slightly lighter background with subtle border treatment and appropriate border-radius. Colors: #22d3ee as the primary accent for interactive elements and highlights. #d0d0f0 for headings, #8a8ab0 for body text. The aesthetic captures the essence of three.js generative through careful color choices, spacing, and visual weight. Set in Space Grotesk — chosen for its visual character that complements this design style. Heading at weight 600-700, 24-28px, line-height 1.2-1.3. Eyebrow at weight 500, 11px, uppercase, 2-3px letter-spacing, color #22d3ee. Body at weight 400, 14-15px, line-height 1.6-1.7, color #8a8ab0. Buttons: primary — #22d3ee background with contrasting text. Secondary: transparent with #22d3ee border. Alternatives: system-ui (universal fallback), sans-serif stack.
